communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your response and for providing the purchase order records.

The list provided appears to consist primarily of general procurement and supply purchases from Amazon and do not address the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on campus.

To clarify, the request sent on 01/15/2026 is seeking records of:

- Contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, work authorizations, or informal arrangements for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities-related work performed on campus, including contracts under $5,000 that are maintained internally by the school.

- Procurement and payment records, including invoices, purchase orders, reimbursements, receipts, stipends, or any other records reflecting compensation or consideration for facilities or construction work.

- Contractor or worker documentation, including business names, individual names, contractor license numbers, proof of license, insurance certificates, bonding documentation, or records reflecting whether work was performed by licensed or unlicensed individuals.

- Internal approvals or authorizations, including administrative approvals or governing board approvals, for construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work.

- Permits, inspection reports, or compliance documentation associated with any construction, renovation, or facilities projects.

- Email correspondence or written communications between school administrators, staff, or governing board members regarding construction, renovation, repair, facilities work, or procurement related to campus improvements.

If no such records exist for the requested time period of January 1, 2023 through present, please confirm that no responsive records exist.

Otherwise, please provide any responsive records or indicate when they will be made available.

As a follow-up to the original request submitted on January 10, 2026, please provide an update on the status of the request consistent with the timelines set forth under the California Public Records Act.

Thank you for your assistance.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Hello,

I am writing regarding my Public Records Act request submitted on 01/15/2026, and follow-up on 04/14/2026.

To date, no response has been received addressing the specific categories requested related to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on your campus.

Under the California Public Records Act (Gov. Code §6253(c)), agencies are required to make a determination within 10 calendar days regarding whether responsive records exist and to promptly notify the requester.

The materials previously provided did not address the requested categories, and no further clarification or response has been received following my follow-up.

Accordingly, please:

Confirm whether responsive records related to the request exist, and
Provide any responsive records, or a clear statement that no responsive records exist.

If additional time is required to produce records, please provide a specific date by which responsive records will be made available.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

communications.subject: Re: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Bennett:

We are in receipt of your request. Please be advised that your earlier
emails were intercepted by the organization's spam filters and did not make
its way to its intended destination. We are now in receipt of your request
and are processing the request accordingly.

We confirm that responsive records exist, however, the nature of the
request appears to be voluminous and broad and is expected to take an
extended time to collect and produce all potentially responsive records as
written. However, we understand from the context and subsequent information
we have received that the request is seeking the below-listed information
specifically in the context of work performed by Done and Done (the
"Subject").

Can you confirm if the request can be considered narrowed as to documents
concerning the Subject?  We make this request pursuant to Government Code
Section 79200.600 "in order to assist the member of the public make a
focused and effective request."  We anticipate that with the scope focused
on the Subject, it would be possible to produce responsive records within
approximately two-three weeks. Any broader scope would be expected to take
a period of months given that it would require a manual search of a
significant volume of records in physical and electronic format by limited
staffing resources (the responding agency is a public school).

This response is without waiver of any privileges or exemptions that may be
claimed as responsive documents are reviewed.

Thank you,
Adriani Leon

-- 

Adriani Leon,
Community Schools Director, Hablo Español!
almafuerteps.org

Alma Fuerte is a public school with a focus on entrepreneurship!
We are a tight-knit and diverse TK-8th community based in Pasadena.

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Alma Fuerte,

Thank you for your response.

This Public Records Act request is not intended to be limited to any particular contractor, company, or individual. While I am not familiar with the specific reference to “Done and Done” mentioned in your response, if that is a contractor, vendor, company, or individual/entity that performed construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work on school property during the requested time period, then records relating to that work would be responsive and should be included. However, this request is not limited to only one specific entity.

To clarify, the request seeks records relating to construction, renovation, repair, or facilities work performed on Alma Fuerte school property from January 1, 2023 to present, including:

1. Records identifying each campus construction, renovation, repair, or facilities project, including location and description of work;

2. Records identifying the companies, contractors, vendors, or individuals who performed such work, including associated license numbers, insurance documentation, permits, inspections, approvals, or related compliance documentation;

3. Contracts, agreements, work authorizations, invoices, purchase orders, or procurement records relating to such work;

4. Communications or records reflecting authorization, coordination, approval, or performance of such projects.

The original request was submitted on January 15, 2026. Despite the indication that prior communications were intercepted by a spam filter, the school ultimately did provide a response approximately two months after the original request. However, the records produced consisted primarily of general Amazon purchase records that did not appear responsive to the categories requested above.

Under the California Public Records Act, agencies are required to make a determination within 10 days of receipt of a request and may extend that determination period only as permitted by law. The Act does not permit delay or obstruction of access to public records, nor does it provide an exception allowing a public school to avoid those obligations based on staffing limitations, school size, or the need to search ordinary procurement and facilities records.

The requested records concern recent construction and facilities work and involve basic procurement, contractor, and compliance records that would ordinarily be maintained in the regular course of school operations. Given that the original request was submitted four months ago, and the school has already had substantial time to identify and produce responsive records, an open-ended production timeline extending several additional months is not reasonable.

If certain items require additional time to compile, responsive records should be produced on a rolling basis, beginning with items #1 and #2 above, along with a specific date for the first production and any subsequent rolling productions.

Thank you for your continued attention to the request.

Steve Bennett

communications.subject: RE: California Public Records Act Request: Facilities Construction and Procurement Records (2023–Present)
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Leon,

I am following up regarding my 05/13/2026 message re-clarifying the scope of this Public Records Act request initially made on 01/15/2026 and requesting dates for rolling production of responsive records.

As of today, 05/21/2026, no further response, production timeline, or rolling production date has been received.

Please provide:

1. A date for the first rolling production of responsive records; and
2. An estimated schedule for any subsequent productions.

Thank you.

Best regards,
Steve Bennett

communications.communication:

RE: Reference # P005183-113025 Dear Beyonce Salvador, The City of Las Cruces received the following public records request from you on December 01, 2025: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the New Mexico Inspection of Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

I am requesting all records related to the arrests of Elijah Ray Chavez on 
9/1/24 near 2125 S ESPINA ST, Dona Ana, NM, 
1/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA ST D11, Dona Ana, NM 
4/10/25 near E UNIVERSITY AVE/LOCUST, Dona Ana, NM, 
7/9/25 near 1410 S SOLANO, Dona Ana, NM, 
8/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA #11, Dona Ana, NM 
and 9/5/2025 near EPDC ANNEX, Dona Ana, NM 
by the Las Cruces Police Department (LCPD), NMSU Police, and DASO. 

I am specifically requesting: 

The complete incident and arrest reports, including officer narratives and any probable cause affidavits 

Any body-worn camera (BWC) footage from responding officers 

Any 911 call audio and transcripts 

CAD logs, dispatch records, or radio traffic related to the incident 

Any photographs, witness statements, or scene documentation 

This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency in domestic incident responses and the handling of battery cases involving household members. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Beyonce Salvador 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2143405/ 
The purpose of this message is to provide you with a notification relating to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). Under NMSA 1978, § 14-2-8, the City has determined that it does not have possession of and/or responsibility for the 911 call audio and transcripts, CAD logs, radio traffic, or any other dispatch records that you have requested.  However, the City knows the proper custodian of these records to be Mesilla Valley Regional Dispatch Authority (MVRDA), 911 Lake Tahoe Ct., Las Cruces, NM, 88007, (575) 526-0795, https://www.mvrda.org/records-request-instructions/.  As such, on December 1, 2025, the City forwarded your request to MVRDA.  To expedite your request, you may consider submitting an additional records request to that agency's records custodian directly.  In light of this notification, the City considers the request for dispatch records closed.  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) 1978, § 14-2-8(C) states, in pertinent part, that a “written request . . .shall identify the records sought with reasonable particularity.” The purpose of this requirement is to provide governmental agencies with the information necessary to accurately and fully respond to public record requests. Upon review, the City has determined that your request, as currently written, is too broad to process effectively. In order to facilitate a thorough and accurate response, the City will separate each case referenced in your request into individual requests. This approach allows us to address each case independently and ensures a more efficient and complete response. 

Accordingly, this request is now closed. Please refer to the respective requests for each case moving forward. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

communications.subject: RE: IPRA Request P005183-113025 - Received
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The Mesilla Valley Regional Dispatch Authority has received your public records request to inspect certain records as stated below.

Your request has been entered into our new request portal, NextRequest, and given case #25-248 for tracking purposes. You will receive an e-mail invite shortly from NextRequest. You will be prompted to register, create an account, or log-in if you're already an established user.

You will be contacted within three (3) business days upon receipt of this request.

[cid:image002.png@01DC62D7.8545B120]
Kristin Holguin
Records and IPRA Analyst
Mesilla Valley Regional Dispatch Authority
Non-Emergency Dispatch: 575-526-0795
E: kholguin@mvrda911.gov<mailto:kholguin@mvrda911.gov>
W: www.mvrda911.gov<http://www.mvrda911.gov/>

After reviewing your request, conducting a search and review of responsive records, we have located: one (1) CAD report and one (1) audio file. A portion of responsive record(s) has been redacted due to protected information exempt that is exempt from disclosure pursuant to: NM Stat §14-2-1.1 for personal identifier information. Responsive records have been released to you. Please see the list below for additional details on no records found for specific incidents/additional information needed: * 9/1/24 near 2125 S ESPINA ST, Dona Ana, NM, (No records found) * 1/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA ST D11, Dona Ana, NM (No records found) * 4/10/25 near E UNIVERSITY AVE/LOCUST, Dona Ana, NM, (Multiple incidents for this day and location. Please provide an approximate time.) * 7/9/25 near 1410 S SOLANO, Dona Ana, NM, (No records found) * 8/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA #11, Dona Ana, NM and (No records found) * 9/5/2025 near EPDC ANNEX, Dona Ana, NM by the Las Cruces Police Department (LCPD), NMSU Police, and DASO. (No records found) MVRDA considers your request complete. * LCPD2025086829CAD_Redacted.pdf * LCPD2025086829.zip Document links are valid for one month. communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Police Records Request, Reference # P005228-120125 Dear Beyonce Salvador, The City of Las Cruces received the following public records request from you on December 01, 2025: Original Request P005183-113025 

I am requesting all records related to the arrests of Elijah Ray Chavez on 
7/9/25 near 1410 S SOLANO, Dona Ana, NM, 

I am specifically requesting: 

The complete incident and arrest reports, including officer narratives and any probable cause affidavits 

Any body-worn camera (BWC) footage from responding officers 

Any 911 call audio and transcripts 

CAD logs, dispatch records, or radio traffic related to the incident 

Any photographs, witness statements, or scene documentation 

This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency in domestic incident responses and the handling of battery cases involving household members. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Beyonce Salvador 
The purpose of this message is to provide you with a partial response to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). The City has gathered, reviewed, and appropriately redacted the following public records associated with your request: 

Nine (9) documents 
 
  *  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1.1 states “Protected personal identifier information contained in public records may be redacted by a public body before inspection or copying of a record.” The term “protected personal identifier information” is defined within NMSA Section 14-2-6(F) to include:  (1) all but the last four digits of a: (a) taxpayer identification number;  (b) financial account number; (c) credit or debit card number; or (d) driver’s license number;  (2) all but the year of a person’s date of birth; (3) a social security number; and (4) with regard to a nonelected employee of a public body in the context of the person’s employment, the employee’s nonbusiness home street address, but not the city, state or zip code;  therefore, any of the above-described “protected personal identifier information” found within the responsive records have been redacted. New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1(N) states “[e]very person has a right to inspect public records except as otherwise provided by law.” NMSA Section 31-26-4(A) states that “A victim shall have the right to be treated with fairness and respect for the victim's dignity and privacy throughout the criminal justice process.” The term “Victim” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to mean “an individual against whom a criminal offense is committed. "Victim" also means a family member or a victim's representative when the individual against whom a criminal offense was committed is a minor, is incompetent or is a homicide victim. The term “criminal offense” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to include twenty-one (21) specific crimes. Therefore, any personal information associated within Victim(s) as defined by the Victim’s Rights Act have been redacted. A download link with the contents of this request has been e-mailed to you from AXON.  This download link expires in three (3) days. Please contact our office by replying to this email or via telephone if you have any issues accessing the documents or if you wish to receive the documents in another format. Pursuant to NMSA 1978, § 14-2-10, the City has determined that your written request is excessively burdensome and/or broad. More specifically, the City has determined that it requires an additional reasonable period of time to gather, review, and appropriately redact any and all public records associated with your request.  Although the City cannot anticipate when the remainder of the responsive records associated with your request will become available, the City will produce them within a reasonable period of time. Due to a high volume of requests, the City will be providing rolling disclosures associated with this request. Further, the City will provide a written status update regarding your request within thirty (30) days. While the City may be able to process a portion of your request before that time, we are not guaranteeing this result. We appreciate your patience and understanding in this matter. 

Christine Rivera, Records Custodian, is the official responsible for any redactions and/or denial of your request. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Should you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact my office. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

communications.subject: [Records Center] Police Records Request :: P005229-120125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 
RE: Police Records Request, Reference # P005229-120125 Dear Beyonce Salvador, The City of Las Cruces received the following public records request from you on December 01, 2025: Original Request P005183-113025 

I am requesting all records related to the arrests of Elijah Ray Chavez on 
8/9/25 near 2306 S ESPINA #11, Dona Ana, NM 

I am specifically requesting: 

The complete incident and arrest reports, including officer narratives and any probable cause affidavits 

Any body-worn camera (BWC) footage from responding officers 

Any 911 call audio and transcripts 

CAD logs, dispatch records, or radio traffic related to the incident 

Any photographs, witness statements, or scene documentation 

This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency in domestic incident responses and the handling of battery cases involving household members. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Beyonce Salvador 
The purpose of this message is to provide you with a response to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”).  T he City has gathered, reviewed, and appropriately redacted the following public records associated with your request: 
seven (7) documents, six (6) videos, and seven (7) image files 

 
  *  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1.1 states “Protected personal identifier information contained in public records may be redacted by a public body before inspection or copying of a record.” The term “protected personal identifier information” is defined within NMSA Section 14-2-6(F) to include:  (1) all but the last four digits of a: (a) taxpayer identification number;  (b) financial account number; (c) credit or debit card number; or (d) driver’s license number;  (2) all but the year of a person’s date of birth; (3) a social security number; and (4) with regard to a nonelected employee of a public body in the context of the person’s employment, the employee’s nonbusiness home street address, but not the city, state or zip code;  therefore, any of the above-described “protected personal identifier information” found within the responsive records have been redacted. New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1(N) states “[e]very person has a right to inspect public records except as otherwise provided by law.” NMSA Section 31-26-4(A) states that “A victim shall have the right to be treated with fairness and respect for the victim's dignity and privacy throughout the criminal justice process.” The term “Victim” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to mean “an individual against whom a criminal offense is committed. "Victim" also means a family member or a victim's representative when the individual against whom a criminal offense was committed is a minor, is incompetent or is a homicide victim. The term “criminal offense” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to include twenty-one (21) specific crimes. Therefore, any personal information associated within Victim(s) as defined by the Victim’s Rights Act have been redacted. A download link with the contents of this request has been e-mailed to you from AXON.  This download link expires in three (3) days. Please contact our office by replying to this email or via telephone if you have any issues accessing the documents. Additionally, p lease log in to the Records Request Portal at the following link to retrieve the responsive records, one (1) document file: Police Records Request - P005229-120125 Christine Rivera, Records Custodian, is the official responsible for any redactions and/or denial of your request. The City considers its response to your request complete. 

Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

I am requesting all records related to the arrests of Elijah Ray Chavez on 
7/9/25 near 1410 S SOLANO, Dona Ana, NM, 

I am specifically requesting: 

The complete incident and arrest reports, including officer narratives and any probable cause affidavits 

Any body-worn camera (BWC) footage from responding officers 

Any 911 call audio and transcripts 

CAD logs, dispatch records, or radio traffic related to the incident 

Any photographs, witness statements, or scene documentation 

This request is made in the public interest to promote transparency in domestic incident responses and the handling of battery cases involving household members. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

Sincerely, 

Beyonce Salvador 
The purpose of this message is to provide you with a partial response to the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). The City has gathered, reviewed, and appropriately redacted the following public records associated with your request: 

Two (2) videos 
 
  *  New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1.1 states “Protected personal identifier information contained in public records may be redacted by a public body before inspection or copying of a record.” The term “protected personal identifier information” is defined within NMSA Section 14-2-6(F) to include:  (1) all but the last four digits of a: (a) taxpayer identification number;  (b) financial account number; (c) credit or debit card number; or (d) driver’s license number;  (2) all but the year of a person’s date of birth; (3) a social security number; and (4) with regard to a nonelected employee of a public body in the context of the person’s employment, the employee’s nonbusiness home street address, but not the city, state or zip code;  therefore, any of the above-described “protected personal identifier information” found within the responsive records have been redacted. New Mexico Statutes Annotated (hereinafter “NMSA”) Section 14-2-1(N) states “[e]very person has a right to inspect public records except as otherwise provided by law.” NMSA Section 31-26-4(A) states that “A victim shall have the right to be treated with fairness and respect for the victim's dignity and privacy throughout the criminal justice process.” The term “Victim” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to mean “an individual against whom a criminal offense is committed. "Victim" also means a family member or a victim's representative when the individual against whom a criminal offense was committed is a minor, is incompetent or is a homicide victim. The term “criminal offense” is defined within NMSA 31-26-3(F) to include twenty-one (21) specific crimes. Therefore, any personal information associated within Victim(s) as defined by the Victim’s Rights Act have been redacted. A download link with the contents of this request has been e-mailed to you from AXON. This download link expires in three (3) days. Please contact our office by replying to this email or via telephone if you have any issues accessing the documents. Pursuant to NMSA 1978, § 14-2-10, the City has determined that your written request is excessively burdensome and/or broad. More specifically, the City has determined that it requires an additional reasonable period of time to gather, review, and appropriately redact any and all public records associated with your request. Due to a high volume of requests, the City will be providing rolling disclosures associated with this request. As such, you can expect action regarding this request within thirty-five (35) days. While the City may be able to process a portion of your request before that time, we are not guaranteeing this result. We appreciate your patience and understanding in this matter. 

Christine Rivera, Records Custodian, is the official responsible for any redactions and/or denial of your request. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

The purpose of this message is to provide a status update regarding the Inspection of Public Records Act (hereinafter “IPRA”) request you provided the City of Las Cruces (hereinafter “City” or “Las Cruces”). Due to an unusually large volume of requests, the City is temporarily extending all requests for a period of ninety (90) days. This does not mean that your request will take the full 90-day period. As of the date of this communication, the City currently has a backlog of approximately 1,700 pending IPRA requests, while still receiving approximately 30-40 new requests daily. As you know, it takes time to receive and review each request, document same, gather responsive documents, issue redactions where appropriate, and to disclose the responsive documents to you, the requester. Please note that this 90-day extension policy is temporary in nature, and the City will re-evaluate its position when the backlog returns to sustainable levels, or on July 1, 2026, whichever occurs first. Please note that this is being implemented because the City has received hundreds of requests that pertain to records that are not stored in one location, involving coordination among multiple people, and a significant number of records requests have been received unexpectedly. Normally, the City has adequate staff needs to process its obligations under the Inspection of Public Records Act. However, we are experiencing unexpected exponential growth regarding the number of new requests. The City apologizes for any inconvenience that this may cause, and thanks you for your patience and understanding. 

*If you are an attorney requesting records, please do not utilize the IPRA process to request documents for a matter of pending litigation. The Clerk requests that you instead send a subpoena duces tecum to the City Clerk’s office, copying the City Attorney. Your request will be fulfilled in a more expedited manner. 

Should you have any questions or concerns, please do not hesitate to contact my office. Sincerely, 
Christine Rivera 
Records Custodian 
(575) 541-2112

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Dear Keweenaw County Clerk FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Keweenaw County Clerk county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: RE: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request (Keweenaw County)
communications.communication:
Hi Julie,

Thanks for your April 20, 2026 written response. As I understand your response to be a denial of my FOIA request — since the responsive records are being conditioned on a $1,250 fee not authorized under FOIA — please treat this message as a written appeal under MCL 15.240.

Three things to flag.

1. "Act or statute" in MCL 15.234(10) means a state statute that explicitly authorizes the sale or sets the fee. The Michigan Court of Appeals construed this carve-out in Grebner v. Clinton Charter Twp., 216 Mich. App. 736 (1996):

"A primary definition of the word 'specific' is 'explicit.' … In contrast, the Michigan Election Law, § 522, provides only for the payment of costs of preparing copies of voter registration records, as opposed to their sale. Clearly, this is not explicit authorization of the sale of voter registration rolls."

216 Mich. App. at 743.

MCL 48.101 governs transcripts from the tax roll maintained by the Treasurer's office. It does not explicitly authorize the sale of the Equalization Department's assessment roll, and it does not set a specific fee for such records. The $1,250 figure is a departmental/BoC fee-schedule number, not a fee "specifically provided by an act or statute" within the meaning of MCL 15.234(10).

EAPRA (MCL 15.441–15.446) fails the same test: it doesn't explicitly authorize the sale of assessment rolls, and it doesn't set a specific fee.

2. EAPRA has its own savings clause. MCL 15.443(4): "This act does not limit the right of a person to inspect or copy a public record under [FOIA]." The Senate Fiscal Agency's committee summary for the enacting bill (HB 5832, PA 462 of 1996) puts it plainly:

"The bill would not limit access to a public record under the FOIA. A public record available by enhanced access would have to be made available for inspection or copying in accordance with [FOIA]."

SFA Committee Summary HB 5832 (H-3), 9-18-96, at 2. (Attached.)

3. MI's FOIA has a clear list of exemptions under MCL 15.243. The records I requested are not listed there. FOIA (1976 PA 442) predates EAPRA (1996 PA 462) by ~20 years — if the Legislature had intended EAPRA or MCL 48.101 to make assessment records FOIA-exempt, they would have added them to 15.243.

Side point on custody. I am not requesting anything from the Treasurer's office — no tax billing records, no delinquent tax rolls, no payment receipts, no forfeiture records. I am requesting the assessment roll, which is maintained by the Equalization Department under MCL 211.34 / 211.34d. The assessment roll records valuations (SEV, taxable, classification, PRE). The Treasurer's tax roll records collection. Distinct records, distinct custodians — MCL 48.101 speaks to the Treasurer's records, not Equalization's. I also never requested GIS data.

Requested relief. Pursuant to MCL 15.240(2), I respectfully ask that you:

-- Reverse the denial; and
-- Produce the assessment roll at FOIA cost itemized under MCL 15.234 on the County's cost itemization form.
If this appeal is denied in whole or in part, I reserve all rights under MCL 15.240(1)(b), including a civil action in Keweenaw County Circuit Court with fee-shifting under MCL 15.240(6) and punitive damages under MCL 15.240(7) if the denial is found arbitrary and capricious.

Thanks,
Alexander Munro
awmunro@gmail.com · 734-272-6612
34 N Huron St, OFC
Ypsilanti, MI 48197

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Scott Adams (Dilbert)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Records mentioning, relating to, or generated as a result of investigation or inquiry into Dilbert cartoonist Scott Raymond Adams (June 8, 1957 – January 13, 2026). His death is widely-known and widely reported; as such, privacy concerns under FOIA Exemptions 6 and 7 are voided, as are any concerns under the Privacy Act. For example, you may find his New York Times obituary at the following link: https://www.nytimes.com/2026/01/13/arts/scott-adams-dead.html

Please conduct a search of the Central Records System, including but not limited to the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Indices, the Microphone Surveillance (MISUR) Indices, the Physical Surveillance (FISUR) Indices, and the Technical Surveillance (TESUR) Indices, for both main-file records and cross-reference records of both HQ and all field offices for all relevant names, agencies, organizations, companies and events including but not limited to those cited in the previous paragraphs and/or links as well as a cross-reference with the Southern Poverty Law Center to include any information provided by the SPLC. My request includes but is not limited to 137, 157, 176, 177, 183, 184, 188, and 214 files. If previously released records are available, then I request a rolling release consisting of those records while additional records are located and processed for release.

If any part of these documents are exempt from disclosure, please be sure to review and release all reasonably segregable nonexempt portions of responsive documents, as the FOIA requires.

I request that I be properly classified as a media requester. I am an assistant editor at Reason Magazine who has written numerous articles on topics of public interest, and I am requesting these documents in an editorial capacity. Please contact me if you have any questions about my media status.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

title: Public Records Request: Flock audits (Quincy Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Organization Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. Event Log in Flock
The Flock Safety Event Log for the time period of January 1, 2023, to the date this request is processed.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

e

communications.communication:
Weber State University ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-1022 has been submitted successfully. Your request for copies of documents on file at Weber State University under the provisions of the Utah Government Record Access and Management Act (“GRAMA”) was received. In general, GRAMA requests will be filled within 10 business days as authorized under GRAMA. Should your request fall under one of the categories where GRAMA authorizes an extended time frame for response or where your request is denied as authorized under GRAMA, you will be notified by the end of 10 business days. GRAMA allows a governmental entity to charge fees associated with compiling a GRAMA request. If anticipated fees exceed $50.00, prepayment is required. Any prepaid amount in excess of fees due shall be returned to the requester. You will be notified as soon as possible regarding estimated charges. As the requester, you can always see the status of your request by signing into the Weber State University portal. If you haven't already signed in, you may need to activate or setup your account to get started. Once your account is activated, you can communicate directly with the Weber State University through NextRequest. ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Weber State University.

communications.subject: Kentucky Open Records Act Request: Jeremy stinnett
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Kentucky Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

911 audio of the call placed by [Patricia G. Hook ] on [July 9, 2022 ] at approximately [7:40 p.m. to 7:45 p.m ], reporting [that kathy cann shot Jeremy stinnett  ] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,  Grayson County, Kentucky].

Body-worn camera video and audio from the first-responding officer(s) at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,], documenting arrival and initial assessment of the scene (including confirmation of deceased victim(s), if applicable).


Body-worn camera video and audio capturing [the conversation with kathy cann ] at approximately [8:58pm ] on [July 9, 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,]

Body-worn camera video and audio capturing [Patricia describing the whole incident in the crime scene ] at approximately [9:21 pm] on July 9, 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,]

Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Kathy Cann ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 9, 2022] 



Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Patricia G. Hook ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 9, 2022] 


Full video and audio recording(s) of witness interview(s) conducted with [Jeremy stinnett (Victim of this case) ] conducted by the [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [19 July 2022].


Body-worn camera and/or dash/MVR video and audio from the primary arresting officer(s) documenting the arrest of [Eddie Cann ] on [19 July 2022] at [6600 block of Grayson Springs Road,] from first contact through handcuffing/detention, escort, and placement into the patrol vehicle (including any spontaneous statements captured during the arrest).


Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Kathy Cann ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 19, 2022]


Full interview/interrogation room video and audio recording(s) of any custodial interview(s) or interrogation(s) of [Eddie Cann  ] conducted by [Grayson County Sheriff’s Office ] on [July 19, 2022]

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Danielle D.

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Fall River Police Department)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

title: Community Preservation Committee meeting of December 7, 2023

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Community Preservation Committee meeting of December 7, 2023
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Pursuant to M.G.L. c. 66 §10, I respectfully request the following public records from the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee (CPC), forwarded via the Selectmen's Office as needed:

1. The complete minutes (draft and final) of the Plymouth Community Preservation Committee meeting held on or about December 7, 2023.
2. The complete audio and/or video recording of that meeting.
3. The agenda packet distributed to CPC members in advance of that meeting.
4. All applications, supporting documents, and recommendation memoranda associated with any grant award to A.D. Makepeace Company or any subsidiary or affiliated entity considered at that meeting.
5. The full roll-call vote on any CPC recommendation regarding A.D. Makepeace at that meeting.

If any portion is exempt, please identify the specific exemption claimed and produce the segregable portions. Per M.G.L. c. 66 §10, please respond within 10 business days.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Edward Havey

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0880 : Request Submission Acceptance Confirmation
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. 
Town of Plymouth, MA 
Public Record Request Number:2026-0880 
Requester: Edward Havey 
Request Date: Wednesday, May 20, 2026 7:30:00 AM 
Response Due Date: Thursday, June 4, 2026 

Dear  Edward Havey : 

We have received your public Records request for Town of Plymouth. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Town of Plymouth, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date. 
Thank you. 
Connor Anderson 
Records Access Officer 
Town of Plymouth 
26 Court St. 
Plymouth, MA 02360 
Tel: (508)-322-3388 x10213 
Email: canderson@plymouth-ma.gov

We have completed the work in reference to your
request as referenced above. The response is given
below. Request Response 1. All meeting minutes (which contain votes) and agendas are posted on the Town's website here: https://plymouth-ma.gov/agendacenter 
2. There is no audio or video recordings of the meeting as they have been disposed of in accordance with the Massachusetts Municipal Retention Schedule. 
3. The supporting documentation is attached Attachments 
This
response may or may not contain separate Response Documents to include specific
response and data. In case such separate response documents exist, they are

Town of Plymouth 
26 Court St. 
Plymouth, MA 02360

Email: canderson@plymouth-ma.gov 
If you object to the Town’s response, in accordance with G.L. c. 66, § 10 and 950 CMR 32.00, you may appeal to the following office: Supervisor of Public Records, Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth, Public Records Division, One Ashburton Place, 17th Floor, Boston, MA 02108. Under the Public Records Regulations, all appeals to the Supervisor must include a copy of the original request, any response by the RAO and a statement indicating the reason for the appeal. The requester must also provide a copy of the appeal petition to the RAO. By law, the Supervisor is required to respond within 10 business days of receipt of your appeal. In accordance with G.L. c. 66, § 10A(c), you have the right to seek judicial review of an unfavorable decision by commencing a civil action in the Superior Court.
